rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT ROARK O'NEILL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adjudication Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied, and Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication is granted as to the derivative claims of negligence and breach of fiduciary duty. Defendant has established that Castro terminated the agency relationship with Defendant in July 2009. (UMF #21.) Plaintiffs have failed to provide any evidence to contradict this fact or to raise an issue regarding Castro's authority to do so. Plaintiffs' objection to Defendant's evidence in support is overruled. The statement made by Castro is not hearsay, as it was not offered to prove the truth of the matter asserted, but rather to establish only that Defendant heard it. Summary adjudication is denied as to the claims for punitive damages. The Court's Order sustaining the demurrers of other defendants in this action is inapplicable to the causes of action stated against Defendant O'Neill. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RIVERWALK HOLDINGS, LTD VS. ANGIE H MARSHALL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RIVERWALK HOLDINGS, LTD's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Opposition Hearing required. Defendant must bring to the hearing evidence showing that the $6,000 deposit on August 5, 2014 is payment for contract work performed by her husband. Further, Defendant must provide evidence establishing that her husband's funds are subject to bankruptcy protection. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALDJIA HALTIM-BERCHE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Granted without leave to amend. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. (Ev. Code secs. 452(c) and (h).) Plaintiff filed her government claim on December 19, 2011. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice (RJN) Ex. 1-A.) Defendant sent Plaintiff written notice of the rejection of her claim on February 1, 2012, which included a warning that Plaintiff would only have six months to file her complaint under Gov. Code sec. 945.6. (RJN, Ex. 1-B.) Plaintiff did not file her complaint until November 14, 2012, after the six month deadline had passed. (RJN, Ex. 2.) "The prescribed statutes of limitations for commencement of actions against the state are mandatory and must be strictly complied with¿[a]nd where, as in the statute here under consideration, an action against the state 'must be commenced within six months,' the requirement is mandatory." (Chase v. State of California (1977) 67 Cal.App.3d 808, 812.) The statute of limitations "is absolute, and will not be extended for any reason." (Id.) Defendant's motion is not untimely, as "[a] motion for judgment on the pleadings may be made at any time either prior to the trial or at the trial itself." (Stoops v. Abassi (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 644, 650.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MORGAN TEMPLETON et al VS. ALUMA SYSTEMS CONCRETE CONSTRUCTION, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC. DBA NIBBI CONCRETE's Motion For Protective Order Motion denied. Moving party does not carry its burden to demonstrate sufficient grounds to delay the noticed depositions in this present action where (1) Nibbi Brothers is not a party to the present personal injury action, (2) the case is at issue, and (3) the action is set for trial November 17, 2014. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: SIU HANG WONG et al VS. DRIVEN RACEWAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Points And Autorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT FLORIDA INTERNATIONAL JET SCHOOL INC. Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued on the court's own motion to October 17, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)
